name: Amsterdam
summary: Amsterdam is the capital of the Netherlands, a watery city built around 165 canals and 1,500 bridges. It is famous for its leaning canal houses, art museums, and clattering bicycles everywhere.
funFacts: Amsterdam was built on swampy ground, so its old houses sit on long wooden poles hammered deep into the mud — that is why many of them lean sideways.
There are more bicycles in Amsterdam than people, and even the police chase suspects on two wheels through the busy bike lanes.
Anne Frank was a young girl who hid from the Nazis in a secret room behind a bookcase, and her diary has been read in over 70 languages.
Vincent van Gogh painted swirling skies and bright sunflowers, and a whole museum in Amsterdam holds around 200 of his colourful paintings.
The city's curving canal ring was dug by hand more than 400 years ago and is so special it is now a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
thingsToDo: Glide along the UNESCO-listed canals on a glass-roofed boat to spot leaning houses, arched bridges, and bobbing houseboats.
Visit the Anne Frank House and peek behind the famous bookcase that hid the secret annex during the Second World War.
See Rembrandt's giant painting The Night Watch at the Rijksmuseum, a grand red-brick building filled with Dutch treasures.
Spot Sunflowers and other swirling masterpieces at the Van Gogh Museum, home to the world's biggest collection of his art.
Run, kick a ball, or rent bikes in leafy Vondelpark, with its ponds, playgrounds, and free outdoor concerts in summer.
Catch a quick train to Zaanse Schans to watch traditional wooden windmills spin and peek inside a clog-maker's workshop.

wildlife.name: Eurasian Coot
wildlife.description: Sooty-black water bird with a bright white forehead, often seen swimming in Amsterdam's canals.

wildlife.name: Common Pochard
wildlife.description: Diving duck with a chestnut-red head and grey body, found on city ponds and lakes.

wildlife.name: Mute Swan
wildlife.description: Big white swan with an orange beak that glides quietly across Dutch canals and rivers.

wildlife.name: Harbour Seal
wildlife.description: Spotted grey seal that lounges on Wadden Sea sandbanks north of Amsterdam.

images.alt: An Amsterdam canal lined with arched stone bridges all lit up at dusk.
The Anne Frank House on the Prinsengracht canal next to the Westerkerk church tower.
The grand Rijksmuseum building, a famous art museum that looks a bit like a castle.
The modern stone facade of the Van Gogh Museum in Amsterdam.
The grand Royal Palace standing on Dam Square in central Amsterdam.
Old wooden windmills standing in a row beside a river in the Dutch countryside.
A black Eurasian coot with a white forehead swims on calm water.
A male common pochard duck with a chestnut head and grey body on the water.
A mute swan landing on water with wings spread wide, photographed in the Netherlands.
A spotted harbour seal resting on rocks by the sea.
